rulings.Case Title: WAYNE A. MCFADDEN VS. MARY M. CHIARENZA et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Ntc Of Mtn And Mtn To Compel Compliance
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Tuesday, December 7, 2010, line 1, DEFENDANT MARY CHIARENZA'S Motion To Compel Compliance. GRANTED IN PART. CROSS-DEFENDANT IS ORDERED TO PRODUCE ALL DOCUMENTS RESPONSIVE TO REQUESTS 17 AND 18 WITH "COMPLAINT" DEFINED IN THE ORDINARY DICTIONARY SENSE. "COMPLAINT" MEANS ANYTIME A FORMER OR CURRENT CLIENT EXPRESSED DISCONTENT REGARDING CROSS-DEFENDANT'S BILLING PRACTICES (REG. 17) AND DISCONTENT REGARDING THE QUALITY OF CROSS-DEFENDANT'S WORK, INCLUDING WITHOUT LIMITATIONS ANY ALLEGATIONS OF MALPRACTICE. (REG. 18). REQUEST FOR CROSS-DEFENDANT TO PRODUCE ENTIRE FILES FOR CASES REGARDING THE COLLECTION OF FEES IS DENIED. =(302/CWW)

rulings.Case Title: JULIANNA AGARDI VS. CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO, A GOVERNMENTAL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Plaintiff Julianna Agardi'S Motion For Judgment On The Pleadings (Even Case Number, Set In 301 In Error))
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Tuesday, December 7, 2010, line 9, Plaintiff Julianna Agardi's Motion For Judgment On The Pleadings. GRANTED IN PART AND DENIED IN PART. GRANTED WITH LEAVE TO AMEND AS TO AFFIRMATIVE DEFENSES NUMBER 2, 3, 4, 5, 6, 7, 8, 9, AND 10. SEE FPI DEVELOPMENT INC. V. NAKASHIMA (1991) 231 CAL.APP.3D 367, 384. DEFENDANT MUST PROVIDE FACTS TO SUPPORT THOSE DEFENSES. MOTION DENIED AS TO REMAINDER. =(302/CWW)

rulings.Case Title: IN RE: MICHAEL COOMBS et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Respondent Archibald Cunningham'S Motion For Reconsideration Pursuant To To Ccp 1008(A)
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Tuesday, December 7, 2010, line 11, Respondent Archibald Cunningham's Motion For Reconsideration Pursuant To To CCP 1008(A). DENIED. RESPONDENT FAILED TO COMPLY WITH CCP SECTION 1008. NO COMPETENT EVIDENCE SUPPORTS THIS MOTION, THERE IS NO SUPPORTING DECLARATION, AND THE ISSUES RAISED ARE IRRELEVANT TO WHETHER ARBITRATION SHOULD BE ORDERED. RESPONDENT TO PAY REASONABLE ATTORNEY FEES AND COSTS TO PETITIONERS IN THE AMOUNT OF $1,200 NO LATER THAN 60 DAYS FROM NOTICE OF THIS ORDER PURSUANT TO SECTION 14.3 G. OF THE AGREEMENT. =(302/CWW)

rulings.Case Title: 178-80 CHURCH STREET, LLC VS. JAMES FLANNERY et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion By Pltf To Compel Deposition Of Deft And Responses To Written Discovery, Adn Request For Monetary Sanctions
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Tuesday, December 7, 2010, line 5, PLAINTIFF 178-80 CHURCH STREET, LLC'S Motion By Plaintiff To Compel Deposition Of Defendant And Responses To Written Discovery, And Request For Monetary Sanctions. GRANTED, NO OPPOSITION FILED. DEFENDANT MUST APPEAR FOR ORAL DEPOSITION. DEFENDANT MUST PROVIDE VERIFIED RESPONSES WITHOUT OBJECTION TO PLAINTIFF'S REQUESTS FOR ADMISSION, FORM INTERROGATORIES (GENERAL), FORM INTERROGATORIES (UD), SPECIAL INTERROGATORIES, AND INSPECTION DEMAND, AND PAY REASONABLE SANCTIONS TO PLAINTIFF IN THE AMOUNT OF $1,080. RESPONSES AND SANCTIONS ARE DUE WITHIN 15 DAYS OF NOTICE OF THIS ORDER. =(302/CWW)
